methods.description: Process for collecting sediment core samples from a lake using a Livingstone piston-corer.

steps.description: A 492 cm long sediment core was recovered with a Livingstone piston-corer near the centre of the lake (c. 5 m water depth) from an inflatable rubber raft.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment core using Livingstone piston-corer

steps.description: Core sections of 1 m were recovered in tubes and stored under dark and cold (+4°C) conditions before processing.
steps.searchable_summary: store sediment core sections in dark and cold conditions

methods.description: Procedure for dating organic bulk subsamples using Accelerator Mass Spectrometer (AMS) radiocarbon dating.

steps.description: Four bulk subsamples (organic) were submitted for Accelerator Mass Spectrometer (AMS) radiocarbon dating.
steps.searchable_summary: submit organic subsamples for AMS radiocarbon dating

steps.description: Radiocarbon dates were performed at the University of Erlangen/Nürnberg, Germany.
steps.searchable_summary: perform radiocarbon dating at University of Erlangen/Nürnberg

steps.description: Radiocarbon ages were calibrated using Calib 5.0.2, performed with the Southern Hemisphere calibration curve.
Median ages of the 2σ distribution were used for the age-depth model.
steps.searchable_summary: use median ages for age-depth model

methods.description: Analysis of sediment core subsamples for pollen and charcoal content using standard analytical methods.

steps.description: A total of 52 subsamples (0.25 cm3) were taken from the sediment core for palynological and charcoal analyses mainly at 10 cm (5 cm) intervals.
steps.searchable_summary: extract subsamples for palynological and charcoal analysis

steps.description: All samples were processed with standard analytical methods.
steps.searchable_summary: process samples with standard analytical methods

steps.description: Exotic Lycopodium spores were added to each sample before treatment for calculation of pollen and charcoal concentration.
steps.searchable_summary: add Lycopodium spores for concentration calculation

steps.description: Approximately 300 pollen grains were counted for each sample.
Pollen identification was based on the reference collection of H. Behling and literature.
steps.searchable_summary: identify pollen using reference collection and literature

steps.description: Pollen and spore data are presented in pollen diagrams as percentages of the terrestrial pollen sum.
Pollen concentration (grains/cm3) and influx (grains/cm2 per yr) were also calculated.
Carbonized particles (10-150 µm) were counted on pollen slides and presented as influx (particles/cm2 per yr).
steps.searchable_summary: count carbonized particles and calculate influx

methods.description: Non-destructive scanning of sediment cores for magnetic susceptibility and elemental analysis using XRF.

steps.description: Non-destructive magnetic susceptibility (κ) scanning was performed on split cores with a Bartington MS2F point sensor at 1 cm resolution.
steps.searchable_summary: perform magnetic susceptibility scanning on cores

steps.description: An Avaatech XRF-scanner provided semi-quantitative analyses of Al, Si, S, K, Ca, Ti, Mn and Fe at 1 cm depth intervals.
steps.searchable_summary: perform XRF scanning for elemental analysis
